response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack and backend software engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and web infrastructure credentials, but his profile has no meaningful overlap with the Principal Performance Architect role. The position demands deep expertise in SoC/IP modeling, hardware-software co-design, AI accelerator performance analysis, and GPU/SIMD architecture — none of which appear in his resume. His 'AI' exposure at Stripe is limited to MCP tooling and agentic developer workflows, which is categorically different from deep-learning workload optimization at the hardware level. His low-latency work at Sociomantic and embedded systems mentions are too thin and too web-adjacent to bridge this gap.
strengths: 15+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains, demonstrating adaptability
Demonstrated performance-conscious development (low-latency async I/O, high-throughput HTTP services handling millions of requests/second)
Recent exposure to AI-agent infrastructure and tooling at Stripe, showing awareness of the AI ecosystem
concerns: Zero experience with SoC/IP modeling, hardware accelerator design, or GPU/SIMD/MIMD architectures — the core technical requirements of this role
No background in deep learning, neural network training/inference optimization, or AI workload analysis at the hardware or system level
Education is a BS in Computer Science with no graduate-level work in hardware, computer architecture, or relevant engineering disciplines, and experience does not compensate in the right domain

key_factors.factor: No SoC/IP or hardware modeling experience
No deep learning or AI accelerator workload expertise
Some performance engineering exposure in software
